notes: Camera contract is artistically locked; Flutter is authoritative.
North marks the start of the deep approach; Camp follows the railway past the fields and snowy farms to the base of the forest artwork.

The 1672 x 941 viewport is the legacy calibrated proof space. New Figma raster masters and depth-card registration use the locked 12:5, 1920 x 800 authoring viewport defined by design/field-plan-world/DEPTH_CARD_PIPELINE.md.
The active horizon is registered midway between the upper and lower RM40 forest and snow seam readings at pixel Y 413 on the 1920 x 800 authoring plate, normalized Y 0.51625.
The fixed North base was migrated with the current plate from legacy [836, 394] to current [960, 413].
The earlier normalized Y 0.4 belongs to the 1672 x 941 calibration proof and is not the current RM40 backplate horizon.
RM40's railway terminates inside the camp scene. Its finite rail edges are not an infinite perspective reference and must not be used to derive the camera vanishing point.

All pre-RM40 depth cards retain their 0.40 authoring horizon provenance and receive a one-time +0.11625H (+93 px at 1920 x 800) registration shift. RM40 is already registered to 0.51625 and is not shifted.
